Class SurvislandCommand

java.lang.Object
com.lwidev.survisland.api.command.SurvislandCommand
public abstract class SurvislandCommand extends Object
Base class for every Survisland command. Handles permission derivation ("survisland.<name>", or "survisland.<name>.<subcommand>" for subcommands created via subcommand(String)) and player-only enforcement automatically.

Subclasses describe their command entirely from the constructor, e.g.: 

public PauseCommand(Survisland plugin) {
    super("pause", "Activer ou désactiver la pause du jeu", PermissionDefault.OP);
    executes(ctx -> { PauseManager.toggle(plugin); return Command.SINGLE_SUCCESS; });
}
No .then(...) chaining and no manual registration bookkeeping needed — SurvislandCommands.register(JavaPlugin, SurvislandCommand...) builds and registers everything.

    • onLiteral

      public final SurvislandCommand onLiteral(String name, com.mojang.brigadier.Command<io.papermc.paper.command.brigadier.CommandSourceStack> executor)
      Adds a literal alternative (e.g. a no-arg root behavior variant) at the flat chain's tail.

    • onArgument

      public final <T> SurvislandCommand onArgument(String name, com.mojang.brigadier.arguments.ArgumentType<T> type, com.mojang.brigadier.Command<io.papermc.paper.command.brigadier.CommandSourceStack> executor)
      Adds an argument alternative at the flat chain's tail.

    • subcommand

      public final Subcommand subcommand(String name)
      Declares a subcommand (e.g. "force" for "/skin force ..."). Its permission is auto-derived as permission() + "." + name and is registered automatically by SurvislandCommands.register(JavaPlugin, SurvislandCommand...).

    • resolvePlayer

      public final org.bukkit.entity.Player resolvePlayer(com.mojang.brigadier.context.CommandContext<io.papermc.paper.command.brigadier.CommandSourceStack> ctx, String argName) throws com.mojang.brigadier.exceptions.CommandSyntaxException
      Resolves a single-player selector argument (name or @a/@e/@p/@r/@s selector).
      Throws:
      com.mojang.brigadier.exceptions.CommandSyntaxException

    • resolvePlayers

      public final List<org.bukkit.entity.Player> resolvePlayers(com.mojang.brigadier.context.CommandContext<io.papermc.paper.command.brigadier.CommandSourceStack> ctx, String argName) throws com.mojang.brigadier.exceptions.CommandSyntaxException
      Resolves a multi-player selector argument (@a/@e/@r or a single name).
      Throws:
      com.mojang.brigadier.exceptions.CommandSyntaxException

    • resolvePlayerProfiles

      public final Collection<com.destroystokyo.paper.profile.PlayerProfile> resolvePlayerProfiles(com.mojang.brigadier.context.CommandContext<io.papermc.paper.command.brigadier.CommandSourceStack> ctx, String argName) throws com.mojang.brigadier.exceptions.CommandSyntaxException
      Resolves a player-profile argument (name or @a/@e/@p/@r/@s selector). Unlike resolvePlayer(CommandContext, String)/resolvePlayers(CommandContext, String), this also matches players who are currently offline: for a plain name, it's looked up via the server's local profile cache (usercache.json) first, falling back to a blocking Mojang HTTP call only for a name never seen on this server before.
      Throws:
      com.mojang.brigadier.exceptions.CommandSyntaxException
